achievements.description: GDCU-8957 Local Navigation on News Site Articles shipped to Done — Carlos Blauth completed the full all-geos implementation at 08:59. Editors can now add local navigation to news-site article page types based on product type, verified across all geos and device types.

In Product review queue cleared from 6 tickets (morning) to 1 — Mike worked through the accumulated backlog (GDCU-8955 Cool Tones P2, GDCU-8936 Ace Nav Redesign, GDCU-8806 Age Gate, GDCU-8874 Page Bloat SEO, GDCU-8948 Prize Boxes 5th Box all exited In Product). Only GDCU-8847 VIP Operator List remains.

GDCU-8983 Cosmo Spins Login Issues created and already in QA — Shane McGrath and Dean Ryan flagged persistent login/session problems; Harden Thomas Wallace produced a fix and moved it to In QA by 15:30. Rapid turnaround on a Dean-escalated issue.

Mobile app infrastructure backlog built out — Carlos Blauth created three new backlog tickets today: GDCU-8969 (Create app in GDC Trading Limited App Store Connect), GDCU-8981 (Migrate Firebase project to apps@gdcgroup.com), and GDCU-8809 (Implement Push Notifications via Bloomreach/OneSignal). These address the personal-account ownership risk and unblock the app submission pipeline.
GDCU-8923 Image Carousel Shortcode cancelled — a decision was made today to cancel this backlog feature (cancelled at 16:33).

Mid Week Catchup held (Mike, Chloe Christie, Ciara Carroll, Gabriel Cornoiu, Eoin McCarthy, Shane McGrath, Shalini Kumar, Joshua Smith) — discussed Diana Thurmann's request to extend the Learning Hub to non-EN geos, sign-up fallback for geos with no offers (GDCU-8843/8187), and Ciara flagged tournament leaderboard update needed ahead of WC predictor leaderboard launch.

Ace dark mode decision confirmed — Mike reviewed the navigation bar contrast in dark mode on Ace and aligned with Ciara Carroll and Miguel Migneco: dark mode is kept as-is since the rest of the site does not support dark mode, one inconsistent page is undesirable.

GDCU-8432 Geo Locate Images on Global HP re-triaged to To Do — this HIGH priority cross-functional ticket (10 geos: IT, DE, RO, GR, NL, BE, DK, SE, ES, MX) was touched at 09:00 and moved to active To Do status, signalling intent to progress.
Ciara Carroll published 'Improvement Spec: Claude Design System Folders' in the GDCC Confluence space — early-stage spec for organising Claude design system assets.
Robin John published 'Global Navigation Test Suite for Gambling.com Across 26 Markets' in GDCC Confluence — QA automation coverage expanded to the global nav across all 26 markets via PR #22.
Search arrow bug reported in #gdc-pod (Shane McGrath) and triaged — Chloe Christie directed Shane to raise a Jira ticket. Bug: search arrow broken on site.

Footer bugs flagged by Robin John: (1) GDC SE homepage footer Twitter icon redirects to SE homepage instead of Twitter; (2) GDC AU footer Quick Links section missing. Under investigation in a 9-reply thread.

meeting_summaries.title: Mid Week Catchup — 13th May 2026
meeting_summaries.time: Morning (time TBC — not in Mike's calendar but confirmed via Confluence page and #6amigos Slack)
meeting_summaries.summary: Regular midweek cross-functional sync. Participants: Mike Pett, Chloe Christie, Ciara Carroll, Gabriel Cornoiu, Eoin McCarthy, Shane McGrath, Shalini Kumar, Joshua Smith. Agenda captured on Confluence (6611304650). Chloe raised three items: (1) Diana Thurmann has asked whether the Learning Hub (gambling.com/learn) can be applied to non-EN geos — scope decision needed; (2) No fallback currently exists for sign-up flows in geos with no offers — GDCU-8843 and GDCU-8187 referenced — decision needed on whether to restore the previous fallback or show a 'no offers available' message; (3) All devs to ensure new tickets are created by EOD. Ciara flagged that tournament leaderboards need an update to accommodate the upcoming WC predictor leaderboard — requires design/dev action. Action items section of the Confluence page was left empty.
decisions: Search arrow bug to be ticketed by Shane McGrath (Chloe directive)
Ace dark mode kept as-is — confirmed by Mike, Ciara and Miguel in #gdc-product-led-growth
action_items_from_meeting: Decide on Learning Hub rollout to non-EN geos — Diana Thurmann's request, needs Mike/Gabriel/Chloe alignment
Decide on sign-up fallback for geos with no offers — review GDCU-8843 and GDCU-8187
All devs to have new tickets raised by EOD today
Ciara to drive tournament leaderboard update spec ahead of WC predictor leaderboard launch

action_points.action: REVIEW & APPROVE GDCU-8847 VIP REV SHARE OPERATOR LIST — The only ticket remaining in 'In Product'. Nebojsa Stanic's implementation: VIP users (level >=2 via ktag/AdTech personalisation API) see a custom operator list on UK. Non-VIP see regular list. Filters and show-more must work. Day 5 in product review (moved from QA 08/05). Clear this first thing tomorrow.

IUJ PRIZE WHEEL QA/PROD DISCREPANCY — Mike raised in #gdc-pod at 09:00 that the wheel-slices edit interface is available in QA Admin but missing in Prod. No dev responded today. Chase Harden, Eoin or Gabriel for an answer — Mike needs to update IUJ prize probabilities and is currently blocked from doing so.

WC PREDICTOR DESIGN — TARGET WAS YESTERDAY (12/05). No Jira or Slack update from Miguel Migneco visible today. GDCU-8935 still in Backlog. Confirm with Miguel first thing: has design started / is it done? If not, assess impact on 11 June hard deadline and plan recovery — full Figma design (entry flow, group stage, knockout, outrights, leaderboard, mobile-first, 3 breakpoints) required before dev handoff.

GROUP BETS KO CALL — Design target was Thu 07/05. No confirmation seen in Slack or Jira — now Day 6 past target. Confirm with Miguel: is Group Bets design done? If yes, schedule KO call with Chloe and the dev team immediately — 11 June is the hard deadline.

SPRINT PLANNING PREP — Full team Sprint Planning tomorrow at 14:30 BST (Chloe Christie, whole team). Prepare sprint priorities before arriving: (1) WC work — Predictor (GDCU-8935) and Group Bets, 11 June deadline; (2) Cosmo Spins WC Reskin (GDCU-8392, Ciara, once GDCU-8619 finishes); (3) GDCU-8983 Cosmo Spins login fix likely completing QA; (4) Mobile app backlog (GDCU-8969, GDCU-8981, GDCU-8809); (5) GDCU-8432 Geo Locate Images HIGH needs assignee; (6) WC tournament leaderboard update (Ciara's mid-week catchup point); (7) Sign-up fallback for geos with no offers.

INTERVIEW PREP — Two 1st interviews tomorrow: Thibault Bougard at 13:30 BST and James Whelan at 16:00 BST. Both organised by Liam Caffrey (recruiter), Gabriel Cornoiu also attending. Review Greenhouse interview kits (links in calendar invites) before tomorrow morning. Note: Sprint Planning (14:30 BST) fills the gap between interviews — leave on time.

ROADMAP SYNC WITH CIARA — Tomorrow 10:30 BST (Mike is organiser). Key topics: (1) WC Predictor leaderboard and tournament leaderboard update (Ciara flagged in today's mid-week catchup); (2) WC Cosmo Spins Reskin (GDCU-8392) sequencing — when does GDCU-8619 complete so she can start? (3) Improvement Spec for Claude Design System Folders (Ciara published today). Zoom agenda link is attached to the calendar invite.

LEARNING HUB NON-EN GEOS — Diana Thurmann requested during today's mid-week catchup. Needs a decision: extend gambling.com/learn to non-EN geos. Align with Gabriel Cornoiu and Chloe Christie — is this a sprint item or a roadmap item? Feed answer back to Diana.

SIGN-UP FALLBACK DECISION — Mid-week catchup flagged GDCU-8843 and GDCU-8187: no fallback for sign-up on geos with no offers. Decide: restore previous fallback, or implement a 'no offers available' message. Align with Chloe and Eoin before sprint planning.

COSMO SPINS LOGIN FIX — GDCU-8983 in QA (Harden's fix submitted 15:30). Monitor QA sign-off. Dean Ryan is closely tracking this (escalated multiple times). Ensure QA completes promptly and update Dean/Shane when live.

FOOTER BUGS — Robin John flagged today: (1) GDC SE footer Twitter icon redirects to SE homepage; (2) GDC AU footer Quick Links missing. Verify resolution from the 9-reply thread in #gdc-pod — content fix or dev ticket needed.

GDCU-8432 GEO LOCATE IMAGES ON GLOBAL HP — Re-triaged to To Do today (HIGH, unassigned). 10 geos: IT, DE, RO, GR, NL, BE, BE/FR, DK, SE, ES, MX. Needs an assignee and sprint slot. Raise in tomorrow's sprint planning.

MOBILE APP BACKLOG — Carlos created GDCU-8969 (App Store Connect), GDCU-8981 (Firebase migration), GDCU-8809 (Push Notifications) today. All in Backlog. Sequence in sprint planning: GDCU-8982 (rebrand/rename) must land before GDCU-8981; GDCU-8981 must land before GDCU-8809 (APNs key dependency).

VIC DADSON RETURN — Vic is signed off until 16 May (Friday). No action until Friday. Ensure data engineering handoff plan (Nemanja reqs) is ready to resume w/c 18 May. Brief Colin Brannigan if coverage gap has grown.
IUJ BANNERS DECISION — Jessica asked on 30/04 about banners (GDCU-8928). Still outstanding — Day 9+. Resolve tomorrow.

WC COSMO SPINS RESKIN — GDCU-8392 (Ciara, Backlog, still waiting on GDCU-8619). Confirm sequencing with Ciara in tomorrow's Roadmap Sync. Full reskin scope (symbols, animations, 3 audio tracks, countdown clock, banners) cannot start until operator list redesign is done. Hard deadline: 11 June.

WC Cosmo Spins Reskin (GDCU-8392) sequencing — when does GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign complete so Ciara can start the full WC reskin?
Claude Design System Folders improvement spec — Ciara published an early draft in Confluence today
next_working_day.prep_notes: Review the Zoom agenda doc before the call. Pull up GDCU-8392 and GDCU-8619 current status. The WC leaderboard point from today's mid-week catchup is the most time-sensitive item — treat it as a decision item with a clear output. 11 June hard deadline for all WC work.

Note: GDCU Assemble Standup is also at 14:30 BST on the same Zoom link — likely the same session with two calendar invites.

next_working_day.prep_notes: Most important meeting of tomorrow. Prepare a ranked sprint priority list before arriving. Critical constraints: (1) 11 June WC hard deadline — all WC work must be in dev by end of next week at the latest; (2) Cosmo Spins Reskin (GDCU-8392) blocked until Ciara finishes GDCU-8619; (3) GDCU-8982 must land before GDCU-8981 (mobile). Check WC Predictor and Group Bets design status with Miguel before the meeting so you can speak to dev-readiness.

next_working_day.synopsis: SCHEDULE CONFLICT with Sprint Planning — both at 14:30 BST on the same Zoom link. Almost certainly the same session with duplicate calendar entries. The standup is likely the opening 30-minute segment of sprint planning.
next_working_day.prep_notes: Same session as Sprint Planning above. No additional prep needed.
